summary: Iteration 94 — Nurse workflow overhaul. Backend 5/5 pytest PASS: GET /api/procedures/nurse/consent-cases returns 200 for nurse/supervisor/incharge with correct schema {cases,completed_count,pending_count}, 403 for student, 401/403 unauthenticated; response sorted by (procedure_date, procedure_time); excludes draft+rejected; count invariants hold (completed+pending==len(cases)); each case has all required fields (id, patient_name, student_name, procedure_date, procedure_time, implant_procedure_type, status, consent_uploaded bool, instruments_autoclaved null|object). Frontend UI automation was only partially successful — calendar rendered April 2026 header after nurse login in first attempt (proving the component mounts for nurse role) but: (a) RN-Web pressable Sign-In button in a second run did not fire via JS click, leaving subsequent assertions inconclusive; (b) react-native-calendars day cells responded to JS click (tapped=true) but the selected-date reveal did not appear — likely because the day click path also goes through RN pointer events that JS .click() doesn't always trigger reliably in this environment. CODE REVIEW of the 4 frontend files + backend endpoint shows every requirement (testIDs, role guards, tile-tap-params, filter default=pending, chips, search, consent-status pill, calendar dot colour logic green/amber/red, non-tappable nurse alert View, search-bar hidden for nurse, tab title 'Cases' when isNurse, dashboard ordering Calendar→PatientConsent→ScheduledCases) correctly implemented. Main agent should either (1) self-verify UI via manual browser or (2) treat this as code-review-passed given backend contract is proven.

integration_issues.flow: Automated nurse UI login on web preview
integration_issues.issue: Playwright JS-based .click() on the 'Sign In' pressable intermittently does not fire RN-Web's onPress handler (memoryTokens stays null, /api/auth/me returns 403). First run succeeded (calendar header 'April 2026' visible after login), second run did not. Not a product bug — affects only automated testing. Recommend main agent self-verify in a real browser, or add a data-testid to the Sign-In button so Playwright can use a deterministic selector with real-input dispatch.

action_items: Add data-testid='login-submit-button' to the Sign-In pressable on /auth/login so automation can deterministically submit (pure testability improvement; does not change product behaviour).

OPTIONAL: Have main agent self-verify nurse UI on a real web browser — load /auth/login, sign in as nurse.1@dental.edu/Nurse@123, and confirm: (a) two tiles at top of Home with numeric counts (expected 1 / 16), (b) tapping GREEN tile navigates to /(tabs)/procedures?nurseFilter=completed with 'Completed' chip highlighted, (c) tapping RED tile opens Pending, (d) clicking Apr 25, 2026 on the calendar reveals nurse-home-date-cases-2026-04-25 with 1 case card containing 'E2E Test' and consent-uploaded pill, (e) bottom tab label reads 'Cases' (not 'My Cases'), (f) /notifications has no search bar and cards do not navigate on tap.
critical_code_review_comments: NurseHomeCalendar.markedDates: dot-colour condition `info.allUploaded ? GREEN : info.anyPending && info.any && hasAnyUploaded(...) ? AMBER : RED` — when all cases on a date are pending, `info.anyPending` AND `hasAnyUploaded()` is false → falls through to RED (correct). When mixed, both sides true → AMBER (correct). When all uploaded, `allUploaded` stays true → GREEN (correct). Logic is sound; minor nit: the extra `info.any` check is redundant since we only enter the loop for dates with cases. Could simplify to `info.allUploaded ? GREEN : hasAnyUploaded(cases, date) ? AMBER : RED`.

NurseCasesScreen reads nurseFilter param once on mount AND via second useEffect keyed on params.nurseFilter. If the user navigates home-tile-pending → Cases (filter=pending) → taps Completed chip manually (filter=completed) → then presses back to dashboard → taps home-tile-pending again, the param stays 'pending' in router state, so the second useEffect correctly resets to pending. Good.

notifications.tsx mixes React Native props (`testID`) with DOM-attribute `data-testid` on the same file (search input uses `data-testid` for the non-nurse branch, nurse-alert uses `testID`). Works because RN-Web maps `testID` → `data-testid`, but mixing styles is inconsistent. Prefer `testID` everywhere for RN cross-platform parity.
/api/procedures/nurse/consent-cases exposes only the fields consumed by the UI — good. Consider indexing `procedures` on {status, archived} for faster query when the dataset grows.

Backend endpoint has `archived: {$ne: True}` guard which is NOT in the spec — consistent with other endpoints in the file but worth documenting that archived cases are silently excluded from nurse counts.

success_rate.frontend: Code review PASS on all 5 modified files; runtime automation partial — 1 of 2 nurse login attempts succeeded enough to render the calendar, but full click-path coverage blocked by RN-Web pressable + JS-click incompatibility in this environment.

test_credentials: nurse.1@dental.edu / Nurse@123; Gaurav.pandey / Student@123; Paresh.gandhi / Supervisor@123; Abhijit.patil / Admin@123 — ALL confirmed via backend pytest.
seed_data_creation: None — relied on existing DB data (1 consent-uploaded case 69cf9bdfdafb502718057bcd on 2026-04-25 10:00, 16 pending per iteration-93 context). Verified live via API: completed_count=1, pending_count=16 for nurse.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend contract is locked in by test_nurse_consent_cases_iteration94.py — re-run anytime. For frontend: the login submit button on /auth/login lacks a deterministic testID, and RN-Web pressables on this preview environment don't always respond to Playwright's JS click. Two workarounds: (A) ask main agent to add data-testid='login-submit-button' to /app/frontend/app/auth/login.tsx submit pressable, or (B) dispatch a real 'pointerdown'+'pointerup' sequence rather than .click() in the automation. First attempted run DID load the nurse dashboard enough to render the calendar (April 2026 header appeared), confirming the nurse role guard and NurseHomeCalendar mount path work correctly end-to-end; the remaining UI assertions (tile numeric counts, tile-tap nav with nurseFilter param, date-cell reveal, chip active state, non-tappable alert View) are strongly supported by source-code review in this report but were not all runtime-verified. Expo is in CI mode — restart via `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` if components were edited.
rca of the issue: No product blocker found. Automation-only issue: RN-Web Pressable ('Sign In') on /auth/login does not have a data-testid, and Playwright's `.click()` on it via text-match occasionally does not trigger React Native's synthetic onPress (RN-Web listens for specific pointer events; .click() dispatches a `click` event which RN-Web Pressable treats as a fallback that is unreliable with JS synthetic events). Mitigation: add testID to login submit button.
